source_milestone: R15 Knowledge Base, Agent Identity, Memory, and RACI Foundations

classification_classes.description: Highest product doctrine or repo-truth authority for current posture.
Governance or operating model that defines how work should proceed.
Domain concept or target model guidance that informs future implementation.
Repo-local executable tooling, validator, renderer, generator, or wrapper.
Focused automated test or gate test for a bounded contract or status surface.
Human-readable current operational truth surface such as README, ACTIVE_STATE, KANBAN, or decision log.
Bounded proof-review or evidence package root that may contain mixed evidence and operator artifacts.
Committed machine-readable evidence such as command manifests, JSON results, imported run packets, or validation outputs.
Generated Markdown or human-facing operator artifact created for review or communication.
Narrative report, audit brief, closeout brief, or planning brief.
Reusable template for report, packet, or documentation shape.
Historical context preserved for audit, comparison, or lineage.
Material that should not guide new decisions except for audit or migration history.
Proposed or experimental material that is not current authority.
Possibly stale, duplicate, or unused material that requires verification and explicit approval before cleanup.
External board, issue, or mirror reference that is not canonical repo truth unless future governance changes that rule.
External replay or remote execution evidence with concrete run identity, artifact identity, digest, and observed refs.
Artifact that needs classification and must carry an explicit reason until resolved.

evidence_kinds.description: Committed code, script, contract, or workflow definition.
Committed machine-readable evidence tied to repository refs or validation output.
Generated artifact, often Markdown or report-like output.
Human-facing operator artifact or narrative packet.
External replay or remote runner evidence with concrete identity and artifact references.
Current status document or board-like status surface.
Planning-only document, recommendation, or future direction.
Command and result manifest for a bounded validation set.
Focused test execution result or machine-verifiable test output.
Evidence kind is not yet known and requires explicit classification reason.

authority_kinds.description: Highest current product doctrine or repo truth authority.
Authority for operating-loop, role, or process behavior.
Authority for validation shape, schema, or tool contract.
Authority for current operational status surface.
Authority for proof, validation, command, or evidence sufficiency.
Context for future planned work only.
Narrative report context that is not proof by itself.
Reference-only material that must not be treated as current authority.
Authority kind is not yet known and requires explicit classification reason.

lifecycle_states.description: Current and usable within its stated authority boundary.
Active work or active milestone/task surface.
Deprecated and not valid for new decisions.
Possible cleanup target pending verification and approval.
Lifecycle state is unknown and requires explicit reason.

proof_status_values.description: The artifact is not proof by itself.
The artifact may support proof by itself when its evidence kind and refs satisfy the taxonomy rules.
The artifact requires machine evidence before supporting proof claims.
The artifact requires external evidence identity before supporting external proof claims.
The artifact is operator-readable context only.
The artifact can be used for history or lineage only.

invalid_state_rules.description: The validator rejects missing required classes, evidence kinds, authority kinds, lifecycle states, and proof statuses.
Class, evidence, authority, lifecycle, and proof-status identifiers must be unique.
Unknown classification, evidence, authority, or lifecycle values require an explicit reason.
Generated operator artifacts and generated Markdown cannot be marked proof_by_itself_true.
Reports may explain evidence but cannot be proof by themselves.
Status surfaces record current posture but do not prove execution by themselves.
External mirrors cannot become canonical repo truth without future governance.
Cleanup candidates require verification and explicit approval before deletion or deprecation.

Positive claims of product runtime, board runtime, external sync, integrations, true multi-agent execution, persistent memory, solved Codex reliability, solved compaction, or R16 opening fail validation.

claims: R15-002 defines an artifact classification taxonomy contract.
R15-002 adds a local taxonomy validator module, CLI wrapper, valid and invalid fixtures, focused tests, a committed taxonomy artifact, and validation manifest.
R15-002 updates status surfaces for R15 active through R15-002 only.

R15-002 implements product runtime, true multi-agent execution, persistent memory engine, Linear implementation, Symphony implementation, GitHub Projects implementation, and custom board implementation.
non_claims: no full repo artifacts classified by R15-002
no repo knowledge index implemented by R15-002
no artifact registry engine implemented by R15-002
no knowledge base implemented by R15-002
no deprecated files cleaned by R15-002
no cleanup decisions approved by R15-002
no agent identity packets implemented by R15-002
no memory scopes implemented by R15-002
no RACI matrix implemented by R15-002
no card re-entry packets implemented by R15-002
no classification or re-entry dry run executed by R15-002
no final R15 proof package complete by R15-002
no product runtime
no board runtime
no external board sync
no Linear implementation
no Symphony implementation
no GitHub Projects implementation
no custom board implementation
no true multi-agent execution
no persistent memory engine
no R16 opening
no solved Codex compaction
no solved Codex reliability
